{{Quote|I can see your orders are all properly drawn, but I have never appreciated Intelligence operatives meddling in fleet affairs.|Admiral Devlia|<ref name="X:RS">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref>}}
'''Devlia''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as an officer with the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He came out of retirement after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] to help the struggling [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He was regarded as short-sighted and arrogant by his superiors, and was blamed for the failure of an attempt to destroy [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] during a surprise [[Commando/Legends|commando]] assault on their base at [[Talasea]].

==Biography==
Devlia was a short, plump man with gray hair and a gray mustache. With his rank of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]], he was placed in charge of the defense of the [[Rachuk sector/Legends|Rachuk sector]], and established his base, located on [[Grand Isle]], within a hollowed-out volcano on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Vladet]], the Rachuk sector's capital. The [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]] [[Corusca Rainbow|''Black Asp'']] was one of the ships under his command. Like most pre-Endor Imperial officers, Devlia possessed an ingrained bias against [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] and [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Humans]], as indicated by his discomfort in communicating with the ''Black Asp''{{'s}} [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Uwlla Iillor]].

===Reputation and weaknesses===
Devlia was not respected by his Imperial superiors, the [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] agent [[Kirtan Loor]] in particular, who recognized in him major weaknesses such as vanity and an overdeveloped sense of superiority. This tended to result in the Admiral underestimating the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]], both of which he considered to be inferior and under-trained.

Devlia was also distrustful of Imperial Intelligence and its head, [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] [[Ysanne Isard]]. This ultimately contributed to his undoing, as his disregard for the advice given him by Intelligence resulted in a failed assault against Rogue Squadron while it was based on the fog-bound planet of Talasea and led to a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] counterstrike that ended in his death, along with the destruction of his base of operations.

===First encounter with Rogue Squadron===
Devlia first encountered Rogue Squadron, which had recently been reformed by [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] leader [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], in [[6 ABY/Legends|6.5 ABY]] when the squadron's [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]] encountered ''Black Asp'' in the [[Chorax system/Legends|Chorax system]]. The squadron disrupted an attempt by the ''Asp'' to capture the ''[[Pulsar Skate]]'', a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] ship captained by [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax Terrik]] that had been running supplies to the New Republic.

Uwlla Iillor, the vessel's commander, determined from communication intercepts and visual data that the X-wings belonged to Rogue Squadron. Although Devlia assumed the strike was a planned ambush against the ''Black Asp'', Uwlla and Imperial Intelligence Agent Loor correctly deduced that the starfighters were accidentally pulled from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] by the ''Asp''{{'s}} [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]]. Devlia reluctantly agreed to work with Loor, who was charged by Director Isard with the destruction of Rogue Squadron. The admiral agreed to send units to find and destroy the squadron base if Loor could isolate it, which Devlia described as "impossible." Soon, however, Loor determined that the Rogue Squadron training base was located on Talasea.<ref name="X:RS" />

===Failed assault and subsequent counterstrike===
{{Quote|If it had been done my way, Admiral Devlia would be a hero, instead of just dead.|Kirtan Loor|<ref name="X:RS" />}}
Although Loor advised Devlia to send a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] followed by a full-scale attack, the admiral did not entirely trust the accuracy of the Imperial Intelligence and instead sent only a single [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] platoon to seek out and attack the base. Devlia felt probe droids were more valuable than stormtroopers, which he described as "a renewable resource."<ref name="X:RS" />

Upon indeed finding the base, the platoon attempted to destroy it themselves by killing Rogue pilots in their sleep and planting bombs at the base. Although the covert assault resulted in the death of six Rogue sentries and [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Lujayne Forge]], all of the Imperial commandos were captured or killed. The [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|stormtrooper transport]] craft was also captured.

As a result of the failed assault, Rogue Squadron retaliated by striking Grand Isle. Devlia presumed the Vladet base was adequately protected by a shield, but Rogue Squadron slipped underneath the shield by blowing a hole in the side of the volcano and piloting the fighters through the hole to destroy the base. Grand Isle was destroyed and Devlia was killed.

Kirtan Loor posthumously described him as an "idiot" who "got what he deserved" for failing to heed his advice regarding the Talasea strike. Loor was also confident that had Devlia not been killed during the counterstrike, that he would have been executed by Ysanne Isard for mishandling the assault against Rogue Squadron.

Isard also posthumously described Devlia as "stupid."<ref name="X:RS" />
